artifacts.name: Kampilan
artifacts.description: The Kampilan is a heavy, single-edged long sword traditionally used by Maranao warriors and chieftains. Its most striking feature is the hilt, which is often carved to represent the gaping mouth of a crocodile or a mythical creature. It symbolizes the bravery and leadership of the person who carries it.

artifacts.name: Kris (Pira)
artifacts.description: The Kris is a double-edged dagger or sword known for its wavy blade, though some versions are straight. In Maranao culture, a Kris is more than a weapon; it is a symbol of rank, authority, and spiritual protection. The intricate details on the hilt and scabbard indicate the owner's social status.

artifacts.name: Panolong
artifacts.description: A Panolong is a carved floor beam that protrudes from the front of the Torogan, the royal house of the Maranao. It is shaped like the prow of a boat and is elaborately carved with naga or floral okir designs. The Panolong serves to protect the house and visually demonstrate the power of the ruling Sultan.
